skill.name: oss-hunter
skill.description: Automatically hunt for high-impact OSS contribution opportunities in trending repositories.
skill.summary: A precision skill for discovering mergeable issues in high-star repositories and generating contribution strategies.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The subprocess.run call uses hardcoded GitHub CLI arguments with no user input injection risk. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are pattern matching errors. The 'Ruby/shell backtick' findings in SKILL.md are markdown documentation code blocks, not executable code. The hardcoded URLs are documentation links. No malicious patterns detected.

content.value_statement: Contributing to open source is valuable but finding the right issues takes hours. This skill automates discovery of mergeable issues in trending repositories and generates ready-to-execute contribution strategies.
actual_capabilities: Searches trending repositories with over 1000 stars using GitHub API
Extracts help-wanted and good-first-issue labeled issues automatically
Generates structured contribution dossiers with repo stats and issue links
Analyzes issue feasibility based on reproducibility and maintainer activity
Filters issues by domain such as AI, Web3, or developer tooling
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated
Cannot submit pull requests on behalf of users
Analysis is limited by API rate limits and context window size
Does not guarantee PR acceptance by maintainers

use_cases.title: New contributor seeking first PR
use_cases.description: Find beginner-friendly issues in popular repositories to build your open source portfolio.
use_cases.target_user: Junior developers learning to contribute to open source

use_cases.title: Experienced developer giving back
use_cases.description: Quickly identify high-impact bug fixes in projects you already use and depend on.
use_cases.target_user: Senior engineers with limited time who want maximum impact

use_cases.title: Agency building community presence
use_cases.description: Systematically find and solve issues in strategic repositories to raise your team's profile.

prompt_templates.title: Find beginner issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Hunt for good-first-issue labeled issues in trending Python repositories with more than 5000 stars.

prompt_templates.title: Domain-specific search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find help-wanted issues in AI and machine learning repositories that were updated in the last 24 hours.

prompt_templates.title: Bug fix focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for bug-labeled issues in langchain-ai and haystack repositories suitable for a quick fix PR.

prompt_templates.title: Full contribution dossier
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete contribution dossier for the top 5 trending repositories in the developer tooling category including issue analysis and fix strategies.

output_examples.input: Find help-wanted issues in trending AI repositories
output_examples.output: Found 12 issues across 5 repositories. Top pick: langchain-ai/langchain Issue #4521 - Add streaming support for custom chains (15.2k stars, high mergeability).

output_examples.input: Generate contribution dossier for Web3 projects
output_examples.output: Generated dossier with 8 opportunities. Recommended: eth-optimism/optimism Issue #789 - Fix gas estimation edge case (8.4k stars, reproducible with test case provided).

best_practices: Read the project's CONTRIBUTING.md file before starting work on any issue
Comment on the issue to confirm you are working on it before submitting a PR
Start with small, focused fixes to build trust with maintainers
anti_patterns: Submitting large refactors without prior discussion with maintainers
Working on stale issues that have been closed or are no longer relevant
Ignoring project-specific coding standards and contribution guidelines

faq.question: Do I need a GitHub account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a GitHub account and the GitHub CLI (gh) installed and authenticated to run the hunt commands.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically submit pull requests?
faq.answer: No, this skill finds and analyzes issues but does not submit PRs. You must create and submit pull requests manually.

faq.question: How does the skill determine which repositories are trending?
faq.answer: It uses the GitHub API to search for repositories with more than 1000 stars that were pushed to within the last 24 hours, sorted by star count.

faq.question: What labels does the skill search for?
faq.answer: By default it searches for help-wanted, good-first-issue, bug, and roadmap labels. You can customize the search criteria.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without installing the GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: No, the skill relies on the gh CLI for authenticated API access and issue operations. Alternative web-based approaches have limited functionality.

faq.question: How do I increase my chances of PR acceptance?
faq.answer: Follow the project's contribution guidelines, write clear commit messages, include tests for your changes, and respond promptly to reviewer feedback.
